sections.name: Phase file inventory and architecture boundary

checks.name: All phase files exist
Repo boundary respected (no engine reimplementation, no ../catanatron edits)
checks.note: No edits outside repo were performed by this audit

checks.name: Model/training features based on PublicState + EncodedAction
Raw catanatron imports confined to expected files
pytest -q passes
pytest tests/test_self_play.py tests/test_policy_value.py -q passes
target_policy is normalized visit distribution (not one-hot)
target_value reflects acting-player perspective
encoded actions / action features / target_policy align index-by-index
No raw Catanatron objects serialized in samples
Self-play produced nontrivial sample count
Mask sums match legal-action counts
Padded actions get zero probability after masking
Padded target_policy entries are zero
Variable-length batching aligns without shape errors
Model forward supports variable legal-action counts
Model can overfit tiny dataset
Policy loss drops substantially
Value loss drops substantially
Checkpoint reload preserves outputs on fixed batch
Training uses deterministic encoded-action ordering consistent with inference
Saved target_policy aligns with teacher visit counts in same order
NeuralMCTSPlayer scores actions using DecisionContext encoded ordering
Action filtering config/path is consistent between self-play teacher and neural search
Raw simulator access only in expected layers

raw_access_references.text: from catanatron import Color, Game, RandomPlayer

checks.name: Chosen raw action is always from original live playable_actions
NeuralMCTS (priors/value off) matches plain MCTS, or explained
checks.note: diff is expected: NeuralMCTS uses PUCT selection while plain MCTS uses UCT

checks.name: Enabling priors/value changes root ranking or visit distribution on fixed state
Model is actively used in search (non-empty priors recorded)
NeuralMCTSPlayer completes games without crashing
NeuralMCTSPlayer returns legal actions (no illegal-action crashes observed)
No obvious END_TURN spam in buildable states
Arena results reproducible under fixed seeds
Priors are not uniformly flat on most states
Priors vary across states
Value predictions vary across states
Model learns beyond constant baseline
